headline: System down: morning agent failed to run; three critical launchd jobs are stuck or producing nothing.
what_happened: Claude CLI binary missing entirely—launchd cannot invoke `/usr/local/bin/claude`, so `com.alta.morning_agent` never ran today, blocking all context/bias/signals.
Execution harness locked on 2026-06-16 in `--live` mode; processes wrong date, screens 0, writes no fills. Wait-for-fills gate cannot open.
`sync_dashboard_data.py` exits clean but produces stale data (mtime 2026-05-31); dashboard split unresolved.
Four anatomy bugs fixed (similarity floor, dotenv load order, venv Python, Reddit scraper); risk constants ratified in RISK_FRAMEWORK.md.
Shadow gapper logged zero signals today; last two live days (07-13, 07-14) posted +0.0456% and +0.0042% respectively—marginal signal, execution risk unresolved on HYP-105/106.
what_it_means: You cannot trade without the morning agent running. The Claude CLI install is a blocking prerequisite—not optional polish. Once installed, repoint all three plists to `$CLAUDE_BIN` from `.env` (single-source fix), reload, and verify launchd logs. The anatomy fixes are solid (similarity floor alone stopped garbage regime labels), but HYP-105/106 are flagged for look-ahead leakage; HYP-107 shows a real but marginal +5.4% median signal that execution costs (spreads, halts) will likely erase. The goal ladder: first fix the jobs, then validate shadow fills on live data with realistic slippage.
todays_one_thing: Install the Claude CLI binary, then add `CLAUDE_BIN=/verified/abs/path` to `~/quant/.env` and reload the three launchd plists via `launchctl`. Verify `com.alta.morning_agent` runs and logs a signal in tomorrow's morning session. Without this, the system remains inert.
